description_long: This object is a rugged cluster of dark stone blocks, irregularly stacked and tightly grouped together. The stones have a rough, weathered surface with visible cracks, chips, and natural textural variations, suggesting significant age and exposure to the elements. The blocks vary in size and shape, with some being more rectangular and others more rounded, creating an uneven silhouette. The coloration is predominantly a deep charcoal gray with subtle brownish undertones, and the surfaces are matte with little to no reflectivity. There are no visible engravings or artificial markings, and the arrangement appears to be dry-stacked without mortar. This structure could serve as a low barrier or obstacle in an urban park, garden, or as part of a retaining wall.
description: A rugged, irregularly stacked cluster of dark stone blocks with rough, weathered surfaces and no visible markings. The stones are tightly grouped, forming a natural-looking barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view shows the largest, flattest faces of the stone blocks, highlighting their stacked arrangement and rough surface texture.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth and layering of the blocks, with more pronounced vertical stacking and some smaller stones visible at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the uneven, multi-level arrangement and additional smaller stones extending outward, emphasizing the irregular silhouette.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the blocky, angular nature of the stones and the way they interlock, with more visible cracks and chips.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or garden retaining wall
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surfaces show visible cracks, chips, and roughness, indicating significant weathering and age. No moss or heavy erosion is present.
